AUDIT-REGISTRY-001.title: Stamp every cleanup pass in the audit registry
AUDIT-REGISTRY-001.instruction: Refresh file inventory before broad work, then stamp touched files and pass proof before finalizing.
sunset_when: The registry update is enforced by automation or CI
Three consecutive cleanup passes include complete pass receipts

DOC-HYGIENE-001.title: Prefer versioned owners over new markdown sprawl
DOC-HYGIENE-001.instruction: Update an existing source-of-truth doc or registry entry before creating a new doc. Mark stale snapshots historical instead of letting conflicting instructions accumulate.
sunset_when: Docs index and doc_versions remain current for five broad passes

CODEGEN-001.title: Keep generated Dart outputs synchronized during iterative work
CODEGEN-001.instruction: When editing Riverpod, Freezed, json_serializable, envied, or other build_runner-backed Dart sources, keep generated outputs from build_runner instead of manually reverting hash/output churn. Prefer running `dart run build_runner watch --delete-conflicting-outputs` during iterative cleanup loops, and stop it only when the user asks or when the session no longer needs codegen.
sunset_when: Code generation is enforced by CI or a repository-local dev daemon

FIRESTORE-RULES-TEST-001.title: Run Firebase rules tests under the required emulators
FIRESTORE-RULES-TEST-001.instruction: Do not run the Firebase rules suite as a standalone Node/npm test unless the required emulators are already running. From the repo root, prefer `firebase emulators:exec --only firestore,storage "npm --prefix functions run test:rules"`. Treat `connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:8080` or `connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:9199` as an emulator-workflow failure first, not proof that the rules or tests are broken.
sunset_when: A wrapper script or CI-only command makes the emulator requirement impossible to bypass
Functions README and data-contract tracker stay in sync for three rules passes

DESIGN-PRIMITIVES-001.title: Prefer shared design primitives over repeated local shells
DESIGN-PRIMITIVES-001.instruction: Before creating local card, sheet, empty-state, rail, row, badge, skeleton, or section UI, check existing primitives such as CatchSurface, CatchBottomSheetScaffold, CatchEmptyState, CatchHorizontalRail, CatchVerticalSection, PersonRow, RunCard, SettingsRow, CatchBadge, and CatchSkeleton.
sunset_when: Widget catalog shows no repeated local shells outside intentional feature-specific UI

WIDGET-CATALOG-001.title: Keep widget catalog current when widget architecture changes
WIDGET-CATALOG-001.instruction: Update docs/widget_catalog.md in the same pass when adding, deleting, moving, renaming, or materially changing a widget, primitive API, screen ownership model, sliver/tab structure, or reusable design-system role. Do not update the catalog for tiny implementation-only edits that do not change inventory or usage guidance.
sunset_when: A generated widget inventory or CI check makes catalog drift visible automatically

DESIGN-TOKENS-001.title: Keep design-system ownership centralized
DESIGN-TOKENS-001.instruction: Theme tokens, typography, app theme, spacing, radius, motion, icon sizes, and compatibility layout helpers should live under lib/core/theme. Do not add new design primitives under generic lib/constants or a parallel top-level lib/theme folder.
sunset_when: Legacy spacing compatibility usage is either migrated or intentionally documented

RESPONSIVE-LAYOUT-001.title: Avoid hardcoded widget dimensions for content-sized UI
RESPONSIVE-LAYOUT-001.instruction: Treat fixed heights and widths on content-bearing widgets as a layout smell. Prefer intrinsic content sizing, flexible constraints, aspect ratios for truly fixed-format media, and min/max constraints only when they preserve responsiveness across screen sizes, text scale, localization, and variable backend content. Fixed dimensions are acceptable for icons, avatars, progress indicators, toolbars, known-format controls, and intentionally bounded visual assets, but cards, tiles, list rows, and sections should not depend on magic-number heights to look correct.
sunset_when: Widget cleanup scans classify fixed dimensions by acceptable fixed-format controls versus content-sized UI
Dashboard, run-club, chat, profile, and run-detail passes have removed unjustified fixed content-card heights

DENORMALIZED-DATA-001.title: Keep denormalized projection fields in sync
DENORMALIZED-DATA-001.instruction: Whenever source-of-truth profile, run, club, membership, participation, match, review, or notification data is denormalized into another document, the write path must identify the source field, projection fields, and sync mechanism. Prefer backend triggers or callable-owned fan-out over client-side best effort. Add tests proving projection updates when the source changes, and add repair/backfill tooling for existing drift when needed.
sunset_when: A projection inventory lists all denormalized fields and their sync owner
CI or a data audit job checks projection drift for critical collections

DEBUG-LOOP-001.title: Use interactive phone debug logs as feedback evidence
DEBUG-LOOP-001.instruction: When user is clicking through the app on a physical phone, run an interactive flutter run session through tool/flutter_with_env.sh, keep stdin open for reload/restart, and summarize fresh logs in pass proof.
sunset_when: A stable automated device-smoke workflow replaces manual debug sessions

CONTROLLER-BOUNDARY-001.title: Keep product behavior out of presentation widgets
CONTROLLER-BOUNDARY-001.instruction: Widgets may own focus, scroll, animations, navigation, temporary input state, and rendering. Move repository writes, validation decisions, mutation orchestration, and product side effects into controllers, view-model providers, or repositories.
sunset_when: Presentation repository-provider scanner stays clean for five cleanup passes

PROVIDER-SEAM-001.title: Use feature-owned provider seams
PROVIDER-SEAM-001.instruction: If a feature has a view model, controller, or feature-specific provider, screens should use that seam rather than reaching around it to a lower-level repository provider.
sunset_when: Feature screens consistently read view models/controllers and no scanner reaches remain

ACTION-CARDINALITY-001.title: Declare action cardinality before wiring UI affordances
ACTION-CARDINALITY-001.instruction: Every user-visible action must declare its scope key and cardinality: not allowed, singleton, unbounded, or domain-bounded. Singleton actions must render their resulting state instead of duplicate CTAs and be enforced server-side. Unbounded actions must have a UI that supports zero, one, and many items. Domain bounds must be real product constraints, not UI shortcuts.
sunset_when: The action inventory covers all primary callables and CTAs
New feature specs include action cardinality as a required field

STREAM-LIFECYCLE-001.title: Do not idle-timeout realtime Firestore listeners
STREAM-LIFECYCLE-001.instruction: Do not wrap Firestore snapshot streams in Stream.timeout to detect stalled loads; realtime streams may legitimately stay silent after initial data. Prefer Riverpod autoDispose, screen-active gating, explicit refresh/retry UI, and focused tests that advance fake time past prior timeout thresholds.
sunset_when: A scanner prevents Stream.timeout usage on repository/provider realtime listeners
All retained stream timeouts are documented as non-Firestore protocol deadlines

ERROR-UI-001.title: Use branded error surfaces for app-facing failures
ERROR-UI-001.instruction: Do not add new raw Center(Text(...)) full-screen errors or reintroduce CatchErrorText-style compatibility wrappers. Route app-facing data-load failures through CatchErrorState, CatchErrorScaffold, CatchSliverErrorState, or CatchInlineErrorState with appErrorMessage/appErrorTitle mapped copy and optional retry. Keep CatchFrameworkErrorView for Flutter framework crashes only, ErrorBanner for inline mutation/form failures, and showCatchErrorSnackBar for transient action failures.
sunset_when: A canonical app error primitive covers full-screen, sliver, and inline contexts
Existing AsyncValue error branches have migrated away from raw centered text except where explicitly justified

ERROR-CATALOG-001.title: Keep the app error catalogue current
ERROR-CATALOG-001.instruction: When adding, renaming, or removing an AppException subclass, stable error code, error mapper, AppErrorContext, branded error primitive, or repeated app-facing failure category, update the catalogue, naming convention, and decision-tree sections in docs/error-handling-audit.md in the same pass. Add typed exceptions only for stable product/domain errors that need branching, analytics, retry policy, or tests; use mapped copy for one-off message changes.
sunset_when: A generated or CI-backed error catalogue check validates subclass/code/doc parity

FLOW-MUTATION-001.title: Advance multi-step flows only after required mutations succeed
FLOW-MUTATION-001.instruction: In multi-step controllers and widgets, do not change the durable flow step or navigate past the current step before the required repository write or external mutation succeeds, unless the transition is intentionally optimistic and has explicit rollback/error UI.
sunset_when: Controller tests cover failure paths for all multi-step flow mutations
No flow controller advances state before awaited persistence without an explicit optimistic contract

EXTERNAL-SIDE-EFFECT-001.title: Put plugin and platform side effects behind seams
EXTERNAL-SIDE-EFFECT-001.instruction: Store launches, URL launches, share sheets, image pickers, connectivity subscriptions, FCM setup, and other platform/plugin effects should sit behind providers/controllers/services so tests can replace them.
sunset_when: Presentation plugin/platform import scanner remains clean for five passes

TOKEN-DRILLING-001.title: Avoid prop drilling CatchTokens
TOKEN-DRILLING-001.instruction: Prefer CatchTokens.of(context) inside leaf widgets instead of passing CatchTokens through constructors unless intentionally rendering with a non-current token set.
sunset_when: Token prop-drilling scanner remains clean for three passes

TEST-ASYNC-001.title: Use named async test flushing helpers
TEST-ASYNC-001.instruction: Use flushTestEventQueue instead of raw Future<void>.delayed(Duration.zero) when tests need queued provider stream or callback delivery.
sunset_when: Scanner reports no raw zero-delay async flushes for three passes

WIDGET-TEST-001.title: Centralize broad widget test settling
WIDGET-TEST-001.instruction: Use semantic/domain pump helpers or pumpFeatureUi instead of scattered raw pumpAndSettle calls in feature tests.
sunset_when: Only the approved centralized helper-level broad wait remains
No feature-level raw pumpAndSettle appears for three passes

WIDGET-SEMANTICS-001.title: Make custom tappables semantic before testing them
WIDGET-SEMANTICS-001.instruction: Before adding fragile widget finders, give custom tap targets stable semantics, labels, tooltips, or keys that describe real user actions.
sunset_when: Feature tappable scanner remains clean for three cleanup passes

SLIVER-001.title: Respect sliver ownership and NestedScrollView overlap contracts
SLIVER-001.instruction: If a parent owns a CustomScrollView, children should usually return slivers; NestedScrollView tabs must inject absorbed overlap or own the inner scroll view. Collapsible title headers followed by pinned bottom rows must move the title upward as it collapses; do not let sticky search/filter/tab rows visually scroll over and cover the title.
sunset_when: Sliver screens have dedicated regression tests and no known blank/overflow regressions
